MU.headline: Two-quarter beat-and-raise cadence accelerating — Q2 revenue beat guide by 28%, EPS by 45%; Q3 guide implies further +40% QoQ.

shifts.note: QoQ growth re-accelerated from +21% to +75% and is guided to +40% again
shifts.trend: Revenue $13.6B → $23.9B actual → $33.5B guide (YoY: +57% → +196% → implied +250%+)

shifts.note: Pricing power compounding — AI demand outpaces industry supply
shifts.trend: Non-GAAP GM 56.8% → 74.9% → ~81% guide (≈ +24 pts of expansion in two quarters)

shifts.note: Beat magnitude widening, not narrowing — credibility on the raised Q3 guide is high
shifts.trend: Q2 revenue guided $18.7B → printed $23.9B (+28% beat); Q3 now guided to $33.5B (+40% QoQ)

shifts.note: Same constraint language for two consecutive quarters; tightness is persistent, not transient
shifts.trend: 'Supply allocation decisions may impact certain customers' (Q1) → 'Industry supply allocation decisions continue' (Q2) — no easing language added

shifts.note: HBM hyperscaler reliance increasing — single-customer dependency to monitor
shifts.trend: One customer reached 13% of six-month revenue (Q2 disclosure) — primarily CMBU (HBM/hyperscale)

shifts.note: Singapore HBM advanced-packaging fab broke ground; capacity additions from CY2027
shifts.trend: FY26 capex ~$20B (Q1 guide); Q1 capex $5.39B + Q2 capex $5.0B = $10.4B run-rate H1 — on pace for $20B+

shifts.note: First meaningful dividend hike in years; mgmt signaling cycle durability
shifts.trend: Dividend $0.115/share (Q1) → $0.15/share (Q2, +30%)

MU.verdict: Thesis intact and strengthening — beat-and-raise cadence is widening, gross-margin expansion is compounding, and guidance credibility is high. The only emerging risk is HBM customer concentration (one customer at 13% of revenue). Supply tightness language has not eased — bullish for memory pricing through CY2026.

NVDA.headline: DC growth holding ~+66–68% YoY; customer concentration formally disclosed at 4-way >10%; China is now effectively zero, but supply/energy commentary unchanged.

shifts.note: Slight re-acceleration on a full-year basis after Blackwell ramp
shifts.trend: DC YoY +66% (Q3 FY26) → +68% (FY26 full year); Networking +162% on GB200/GB300 NVLink ramp

shifts.note: Hyperscaler dependence newly visible — diligence risk-additive
shifts.trend: Q3 disclosed 4 direct customers each >10% of revenue (22/15/13/11%); 10-K did not previously break this out

shifts.note: China optionality fully impaired across both filings
shifts.trend: Q3 China $3.0B vs $8.1B prior-year Q3; H20 effectively zero by FY26 after $4.5B Q1 inventory charge; Feb 2026 H200 license carries 25% US import tariff
Non-GAAP GM 73.4% (Q3) → 73.5% guide (Q1 FY27); compressed YoY on Blackwell full-system mix but holding within target

shifts.note: Quote nearly identical across the two filings
shifts.trend: Both filings cite data-center / energy / capital availability as the gating constraint on customer deployments — no easing language added

shifts.note: Vendor-financing footprint growing — watch FCF translation
shifts.trend: FY26 10-K disclosed $17.5B in private/infra investments plus $3.5B land/power/shell guarantees to early-stage AI customers; Q3 added $7.5B new DC lease obligations FY26 Q4–FY30

NVDA.verdict: Story still about supply-constrained AI capex. The new disclosures (4-way customer concentration, vendor-financing footprint) are risk-additive but don't break the thesis; China optionality is now fully impaired and should be modeled at zero.

CEG.headline: Calpine acquisition closed Jan 7, 2026 — now 55 GW combined fleet; FY26 adjusted EPS guidance $10.45-$11.45 reaffirmed despite elevated acquisition-year costs.

shifts.trend: Nuclear generation revenue grew from ~$20B (FY24) to ~$25.5B (FY25 incl. partial Calpine); Q1 FY26 revenues $8.04B vs $6.37B (+26%) on first full Calpine quarter
shifts.note: Inorganic step-change from Calpine — organic nuclear growth remains steady +3-5% on pricing

shifts.trend: FY25 capex largely nuclear maintenance; Calpine adds $32B combined debt and ~$1-2B annual capex; Crane restart DOE $1B loan guarantee secured Nov 2025
shifts.note: Credit profile materially changed — watch debt-to-EBITDA trajectory post-Calpine integration

shifts.trend: Q3 FY25: single 20-year Meta PPA for Clinton site; FY25 10-K: multiple AI/data center customer PPAs discussed as pipeline; Q1 FY26 guidance maintained without single-customer dependency
shifts.note: Revenue diversifying from nuclear PTC + merchant power + long-term PPAs across AI, Microsoft, Meta

shifts.trend: NY PSC approved 20-year ZEC extension Jan 2026 (through 2049); Crane (TMI) DOE loan guarantee secured; OBBBA nuclear PTC retained; IRA nuclear PTC still intact
shifts.note: Regulatory support strengthening — nuclear is bipartisan in 2026 AI power context

shifts.trend: FY25 adjusted EPS $9.39 vs FY24 $8.67; Q1 FY26 8-K reaffirmed FY26 guidance $10.45-$11.45 — no revision despite Calpine integration complexity
shifts.note: EPS growing despite lower nuclear PTC revenue YoY — Calpine is accretive to adjusted EPS despite large GAAP intangible charges

shifts.trend: Q3 FY25: pure nuclear play. FY25 10-K: combined fleet 55 GW. Q1 FY26: natural gas (Calpine) contributing first quarter earnings — segment earnings split now ~70% nuclear, 30% gas
shifts.note: GAS + nuclear combination de-risks dispatch economics but adds fossil fuel exposure vs pure nuclear play

CEG.verdict: Calpine acquisition is the dominant story — transformative scale but complex integration; the nuclear-AI data center thesis is intact and strengthening (multiple long-term PPAs, Crane restart on track), but the stock is now a power company, not just a nuclear play. Watch debt paydown pace and integration costs through 2026.

OKLO.headline: Meta Pike County 1.2 GW prepayment agreement (Jan 2026) anchors Oklo's first genuine commercial commitment; order book stable at ~18,100 MWe but still non-binding LOIs.

shifts.trend: Operating expenses Q1 FY25 $18M → Q3 FY25 run-rate $27M → FY25 full-year $139M → Q1 FY26 $44.3M (+146% YoY) as Meta Pike County site development commences
shifts.note: Opex acceleration signals real pre-development work underway, not just LOI signing

shifts.trend: FY25 10-K guidance: cash from investing $350-450M in FY26 (vs ~$30M in FY25) — Meta prepayment funds site development; Q1 FY26 confirms ramp is underway
shifts.note: Meta prepayment structure reduces equity dilution while funding major capex step-up

shifts.trend: Order book: ~15,350 MWe (Q3 FY25) → ~18,100 MWe (10-K, Dec 2025) → stable ~18,100 MWe (Q1 FY26); growth in LOIs slowed — milestone now execution, not new signatures
shifts.note: Pipeline is set; next catalyst is Aurora INL construction authorization and first powerhouse completion

shifts.trend: Q3 FY25: NRC Phase I pre-application assessment no significant gaps. 10-K: Aurora Nuclear Safety Design Agreement approved Feb 2026. Q1 FY26: construction authorization targeted 2026
shifts.note: Regulatory cadence is tracking ahead of some prior timelines; NRC engagement constructive

shifts.trend: Cash: $968M raised in FY25 financing; ~$1.18B at Sep 2025; ~$1.1B at Mar 2026 — net burn $80M over 6 months matches $80-100M FY26 opex guidance
shifts.note: Well-funded through 2027+ commercial deployment; no near-term dilution pressure

OKLO.verdict: Meta prepayment agreement is a genuine inflection — transforms Oklo from a LOI story into a funded development project. The thesis is intact: Oklo has the order book and regulatory path. The risk is execution timing — first commercial Aurora deployment 2027-2028 is ambitious. The stock is pricing significant execution success.

GFS.headline: Revenue stabilizing after 2023-2024 correction; flat-to-slight-growth trajectory with no AI GPU upside — GFS is a mature-node specialty play benefiting from CHIPS Act capex, not the inference scaling wave.

shifts.trend: FY25 revenue $6.79B (+0.6% YoY vs -11% in FY24); Q1 FY26 ~$1.75B (+~5% YoY) — recovery from inventory correction but still well below 2022 peak
shifts.note: Auto/IoT recovery driving sequential improvement; consumer handset still weak

shifts.trend: FY25 gross margin 24.9% (vs 26.4% FY24); EBITDA margin 30.2% (vs 32.9%); factory underutilization persists
shifts.note: Margin pressure from fixed costs on flat wafer volumes; improving utilization in 2026 should help

shifts.trend: FY25 capex below peak-cycle levels; CHIPS Act direct funding ($1.5B preliminary memorandum for Malta NY Fab 8) enables below-market-cost capacity addition
shifts.note: CHIPS incentives are a material tailwind — reduces shareholder capex burden for Fab 8 expansion

shifts.trend: Top 5 customers ~65% of revenue across both periods; AMD, Qualcomm, STMicro, Renesas are anchor accounts; Synopsys Processor IP acquisition (Q1 FY26) adds new IP licensing revenue
shifts.note: Customer base is diversified across end markets (auto, RF, IoT) but limited in absolute growth vectors

GFS.verdict: GFS is correctly positioned as a value-trap / avoid in the AI inference dashboard context: it has no exposure to the AI GPU stack (no advanced nodes below 12nm), flat revenue, and contracting margins. The Synopsys Processor IP deal and CHIPS Act Fab 8 expansion are positive optionality but don't change the near-term thesis. Watch for 2026-2027 as auto/IoT recovery potentially re-rates the multiple.
